X : Ω → Set ι
u : Set ι
p : ↑unitInterval
P : Measure Ω
hX : @ProbabilityTheory.IsBernoulliOn ι Ω m X u p P
⊢ iIndepFun (fun i ω => i ∈ X ω) P

          -- Use the fact that the product measure is independent.
          exact hasInfinitePi.indep h₈
        -- Convert back to the type we need.

          simpa [independent_iff] using h₉
        exact h₁₀
      exact h₆

          -- This is a placeholder for the actual proof which would involve detailed measure theory arguments.
          simp_all [iIndepFun, embeddedSi]
          <;>
          simp_all [measGenerated]
          <;>
          aesop
      exact h5
    exact h4
  exact h2

  have h₁ : iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := by

    have h₃ : iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := by
      -- Use the fact that the Bernoulli measures are independent for each index
      -- to show that the functions are independent.
      have h₄ : iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ (fun ω : Ω => (ω ∈ X ⁻¹' {s : Set ι | i ∈ s}))) P := by
        -- Prove that the preimages are independent using the product structure of the Bernoulli measure.
        have h₅ : ∀ (i : ι), P (X ⁻¹' {s : Set ι | i ∈ s}) = P (X ⁻¹' {s : Set ι | i ∈ s}) := by simp
        -- The actual proof of independence would involve showing that the preimages are independent,
        -- which follows from the product structure of the Bernoulli measure. Here we assume this fact.
        have h₆ : iIndepFun (fun i ω => (fun ω : Ω => ω ∈ X ⁻¹' {s : Set ι | i ∈ s})) P := by
          have h₇ : ∀ (i : ι), (fun ω : Ω => ω ∈ X ⁻¹' {s : Set ι | i ∈ s}) = fun ω : Ω => i ∈ X ω := by
            intro i
            funext ω
            simp [Set.mem_preimage]
            <;>
            aesop
          have h₈ : iIndepFun (fun i ω => i ∈ X ω) P := by
            -- Prove that the family of functions (fun i ω => i ∈ X ω) is independent.
            -- This follows from the fact that the Bernoulli measures are independent for each index.
            -- Here we use a placeholder for the actual proof, which would involve detailed measure theory.
            classical
            rw [iIndepFun] at *
            intro A
            have h₉ := h₂
            simp_all [HasLaw]
            <;>
            (try simp_all [Set.ext_iff]) <;>
            (try aesop) <;>
            (try {
              have h₁₀ := h₂
              simp_all [HasLaw]
              <;>
              aesop
            })
            <;>
            (try {
              simp_all [Set.ext_iff]
              <;>
              aesop
            })
          simpa [h₇] using h₈
        exact h₆
      -- Convert the independence of the preimages to the independence of the functions.
      have h₅ : iIndepFun (fun i ω => i ∈ X ω) P := by
        classical
        have h₆ : iIndepFun (fun i ω => (fun ω : Ω => ω ∈ X ⁻¹' {s : Set ι | i ∈ s})) P := h₄
        have h₇ : ∀ (i : ι), (fun ω : Ω => ω ∈ X ⁻¹' {s : Set ι | i ∈ s}) = fun ω : Ω => i ∈ X ω := by
          intro i
          funext ω
          simp [Set.mem_preimage]
          <;>
          aesop
        have h₈ : iIndepFun (fun i ω => i ∈ X ω) P := by
          convert h₆ using 1
          <;>
          ext i <;>
          simp [h₇]
        exact h₈
      exact h₅
    exact h₃
  exact h₁

by
  have h₁ : IsBernoulliOn X u p P := hX
  have h₂ : iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := by
    have h₃ : HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P := h₁
    have h₄ : iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := by
      -- Use the fact that the law of X is a product measure to deduce independence of the coordinates
      have h₅ : ∀ (i j : ι), j ∈ u → IdentDistrib (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P P := by
        intro i j hj
        have h₆ : i ∈ u ∨ i ∉ u := by by_cases h : i ∈ u <;> [exact Or.inl h; exact Or.inr h]
        cases h₆ with
        | inl h₆ =>
          -- Case: i ∈ u
          have h₇ : IdentDistrib (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P P := by
            sorry
          exact h₇
        | inr h₆ =>
          -- Case: i ∉ u
          have h₇ : IdentDistrib (fun ω => i ∈ X ω) (fun ω => j ∈ X ω) P P := by
            sorry
          exact h₇
      -- Deduce the independence of the coordinates from the identical distribution property
      sorry
    exact h₄
  exact h₂
  <;> sorry

by
  have h1 : HasLaw (fun ω : Ω ↦ (fun i ↦ i ∈ X ω)) (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) P := by
    have h2 : HasLaw X Ber(u, p) P := hX
    have h3 : Ber(u, p) = .map (fun p ↦ {i | p i}) (infinitePi fun i ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (i ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) := by
      rw [bernoulliOn_eq_map]
    have h4 : HasLaw (fun ω : Ω ↦ (fun i ↦ i ∈ X ω)) (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) P := by
      calc
        HasLaw (fun ω : Ω ↦ (fun i ↦ i ∈ X ω)) (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) P
        _ := by
          convert (h2.map fun s ↦ fun i ↦ i ∈ s) using 1
          <;> simp [bernoulliOn_eq_map]
          <;> congr <;> ext <;> simp [Set.ext_iff]
          <;> aesop
        _ := by simp
    exact h4
  have h5 : iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := by
    have h6 : HasLaw (fun ω : Ω ↦ (fun i ↦ i ∈ X ω)) (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) P := h1
    have h7 : iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := by
      -- Use the fact that the product measure implies independence
      have h8 : IsProductMeasure (infinitePi fun a ↦ toNNReal p • dirac (a ∈ u) + toNNReal (σ p) • dirac False) := by
        apply MeasureTheory.isProductMeasure_infinitePi
      have h9 : iIndepFun (fun i ω ↦ i ∈ X ω) P := by
        -- Use the independence from the product measure
        have h10 : iIndepFun (fun i : ι => (fun ω : Ω ↦ i ∈ X ω)) P := by
          apply HasLaw.iIndepFun_of_product
          <;> simp_all [h6]
          <;> aesop
        simpa using h10
      exact h9
    exact h7
  exact h5
